text.title: Stakeholder Management Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Stakeholder Management plus the universal skip/run routing pair (referenced by the decision tree).

rule.statement: Every stakeholder MUST be plotted on a Power/Interest grid with an explicit attitude column (Supporter / Neutral / Resistor); grids missing the attitude column MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Power/Interest alone treats supporters and resistors as identical; the attitude column is the gate against blindsiding by a high-power resistor.

rule.statement: Every stakeholder row MUST have a named owner (single person) and an engagement cadence (weekly / biweekly / monthly / quarterly); orphan rows MUST be deleted or assigned.
rule.rationale: Orphan stakeholders are the source of 'nobody told me' escalations; the named-owner + cadence pair is the only durable contract.

rule.statement: Each SDD transition (backlog → todo, todo → in-progress, in-progress → done) MUST list named approvers; transitions without approvers MUST hard-fail.
rule.rationale: Implicit approval distributes accountability across nobody; explicit approval gates are the only structural defence against rework.

rule.statement: Stakeholder updates MUST be tailored to the recipient's attitude + power; broadcast updates to mixed-attitude lists are forbidden except for canonical metric rollups.
rule.rationale: Broadcast updates trigger the loudest resistor and burn supporter goodwill; tailored channels neutralise both failure modes.

rule.statement: Resistors MUST be re-engaged within 7 days of detection with a 1:1 (not email), with the 1:1 outcome logged on the register.
rule.rationale: Resistors who go silent escalate to executive sponsor blocks 80% of the time; the 7-day 1:1 is the only structural intercept.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` do not ALL hold OR any disqualifier in `## Skip If` fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and document the skip reason in the cycle artefact.
rule.rationale: A minimum-form decision tree must route 'preconditions-not-satisfied' to skip; this rule is the canonical target.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` ALL hold AND no disqualifier fires, the agent MUST run the methodology end-to-end and produce the artefact specified in 02-output-contract.xml.
rule.rationale: Pair to skip-this-methodology; together they form the always-present minimal route.
